whatItDoes: Generate complete HTML meta tags for SEO and social sharing. Supports title, description, author, keywords, Open Graph (OG) properties, Twitter Card tags, canonical URL, and robots directives. Includes a live search result preview.
whyItExists: Creating complete and correct meta tags for SEO and social sharing requires knowing the exact property names and formats for multiple standards (HTML, Open Graph, Twitter Cards); this tool generates all tags from simple form inputs.
whoShouldUse: Web developers, SEO specialists, content creators, and marketing teams who need to generate meta tags for web pages without manually writing HTML.
useCases: Generate meta tags for a new blog post or landing page
Create Open Graph tags for social media sharing previews
Set up Twitter Card tags for link previews on Twitter/X
Generate canonical URLs and robots directives for SEO
Preview how a page title and description will appear in Google search results
Quickly update meta tags when page content changes
instructions: Fill in the Page Title and Description — these are the most important fields for SEO
Add optional fields like Author and Keywords for additional metadata
Fill in OG Title, OG Description, and OG Image URL for social sharing previews
Select the appropriate OG Type (website, article, product) and Twitter Card type
Set the Canonical URL to tell search engines the preferred version of the page
Copy the generated HTML and paste it into your page's <head> section
examples: Blog post: title, description, og:image pointing to the post's featured image
Product page: og:type=product, Twitter card=summary_large_image with product photo
Landing page: robots=noindex for a temporary campaign page
bestPractices: Keep title under 60 characters and description under 160 characters for optimal search display
Always include an og:image with a 1200x630 pixel image for best social sharing results
Use the canonical URL to prevent duplicate content issues across URL variations
Test your meta tags with Facebook Debugger and Twitter Card Validator
Include relevant keywords naturally in the title and description
commonMistakes: Making the title or description too long — they get truncated in search results
Using the same meta tags across all pages instead of customizing per page
Forgetting to add og:image which results in poor social media previews
Setting robots to noindex on pages that should be indexed
Using a non-absolute URL for og:image (must be a full URL with protocol)
faq: Do I still need meta keywords for SEO? — Most search engines ignore meta keywords. Focus on title and description instead.

What's the difference between Open Graph and Twitter Cards? — Open Graph is used by Facebook, LinkedIn, and many other platforms. Twitter Cards are Twitter/X-specific with additional features like player and app cards.
How do I test if my meta tags work? — Use the Facebook Sharing Debugger (developers.facebook.com/tools/debug) and Twitter Card Validator (cards-dev.twitter.com/validator).
Should I use the same OG tags as my regular meta tags? — You can, but it's better to customize OG tags specifically for social sharing with different text that's optimized for social feeds.
